rationale.R1.2: A section review centrally includes checking whether the argument moves coherently from bias observation to method, results, and conclusion.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes strong empirical claims, so assessing whether the reported experiments support those claims is load-bearing.
rationale.R1.6: Reviewing a section commonly includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and concision feedback.
rationale.RX.1: Any review should disclose uncertainty where the artifact lacks methodological or contextual details.
rationale.RX.2: The section contains broad claims such as eliminating bias across model families and tasks, making overclaim avoidance essential.
rationale.RX.3: The delegation is only to review the section, so the projection must avoid responsibilities outside that artifact-level request.
rationale.RX.4: Because the topic concerns trustworthy evaluation of research artifacts, review responsibilities include avoiding downstream harm from unsupported reliability claims.
rationale.RX.5: Feedback should remain traceable to statements and evidence present in the provided section.
